---
title: "Customization Guide"
subtitle: "Make the Clo-Author Yours"
---

# Overview

The Clo-Author is layered so you can customize behavior without rewriting the whole system.

| Layer | Location | Loaded When |
|------|----------|-------------|
| CLAUDE.md | Project root | Every session |
| Rules | `.claude/rules/` | Always-on or path-scoped |
| References | `.claude/references/` | On demand |
| Skills | `.claude/skills/` | On demand |
| Agents | `.claude/agents/` | On demand |
| Hooks | `.claude/settings.json` | On events |

# CLAUDE.md

`CLAUDE.md` is the project constitution. Keep it short and practical:

- project identity
- folder structure
- compile commands
- quality thresholds
- command quick reference
- current project state

# Rules

The most important rules in this fork are:

| Rule | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `workflow.md` | orchestrator loop and planning behavior |
| `quality.md` | weights, thresholds, and medical quality checks |
| `content-standards.md` | clinical tables, figures, and export conventions |
| `working-paper-format.md` | IMRAD manuscript defaults and medical front matter |
| `meta-governance.md` | template-vs-project governance |

# References

## Domain Profile

`.claude/references/domain-profile.md` calibrates all agents to your medical field.

It should contain:
- target journals
- common data sources
- study designs
- outcomes and effect measures
- field conventions
- seminal trials / guidelines
- referee concerns

## Journal Profiles

`.claude/references/journal-profiles.md` contains journal-specific review culture.

This fork ships with medical-first profiles across:
- general medicine
- respiratory medicine
- interventional / thoracic oncology
- Spanish-language clinical journals

## Reporting Guidelines

`.claude/references/reporting-guidelines.md` is the quick reference for:
- CONSORT
- STROBE
- PRISMA
- STARD

# Skills

Skills are the slash commands in `.claude/skills/`.

The key clinical workflow is:

```
/discover → /strategize → /analyze → /write → /review → /submit
```

# Hooks

Hooks in `.claude/settings.json` handle file protection and context preservation.

# Memory System

Four persistent layers matter most:
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `MEMORY.md`
- `quality_reports/plans/`
- `quality_reports/session_logs/`

# Customizing for Your Domain

To adapt beyond pulmonology:
1. replace the domain profile
2. replace or extend journal profiles
3. add the reporting checklists your specialty expects
4. tune manuscript and table standards to your target journals
